title: Arab Bank Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Arab Bank Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Arab Bank Egypt?
faq.answer: Arab Bank Egypt is the Egyptian branch network of Arab Bank PLC, one of the largest financial institutions in the Middle East. Operating as a universal bank, it serves individual and corporate clients across Egypt with a comprehensive range of banking services. Arab Bank Egypt is part of the broader Arab Bank Group, which is headquartered in Amman, Jordan, and operates more than 600 branches across 28 countries on five continents.

faq.question: When was Arab Bank founded?
faq.answer: Arab Bank was originally founded in 1930 in Jerusalem, Mandatory Palestine, as the first private sector financial institution in the Arab world. The bank initially established branches in Egypt during its early expansion throughout the Arab world in the 1940s and 1950s. However, these original Egyptian branches were nationalized in 1961 during the wave of nationalization that swept through newly independent Arab nations. Arab Bank later re-established its presence in Egypt, bringing its extensive international banking expertise back to the Egyptian market.

faq.question: Where is Arab Bank Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: Arab Bank Egypt operates as part of the Arab Bank Group, whose global headquarters are located in Amman, Jordan. The bank's Egyptian operations maintain offices in Cairo and other key Egyptian cities to serve the local market. The parent organization, Arab Bank Group, is a publicly held shareholding company listed on the Amman Stock Exchange.

faq.question: Where does Arab Bank Egypt operate?
faq.answer: Arab Bank Egypt operates throughout Egypt, with branches and ATM networks distributed across major cities and commercial centers. The bank serves clients in Cairo, Alexandria, and other key metropolitan areas throughout the country. As part of the Arab Bank Group's global network, Egyptian clients also benefit from the bank's international presence across the Middle East, North Africa, Europe, Asia, and Australia.

faq.question: What services does Arab Bank Egypt offer?
faq.answer: Arab Bank Egypt provides a full spectrum of banking services including: Retail Banking: Current and savings accounts, personal loans, credit cards, debit cards, bancassurance products, safe deposit boxes, and remittance services. The bank offers specialized programs such as the "Elite" personalized banking service and innovative digital savings accounts like e-Tawfeer, Easy-Grow, and Saving Plus. Corporate Banking: Corporate finance solutions backed by extensive expertise in infrastructure and industrial projects, international transaction banking, SME banking services, and treasury operations. The bank provides working capital facilities, trade finance, and structured financing solutions. Digital Banking: Arabi Mobile app, Arabi Online internet banking platform, SMS Express notifications, card-less withdrawal services, and digital account opening capabilities. The bank also supports the Instant Payment Network (IPN) for convenient money transfers. Additional Services: 24-hour ATM banking, customer care center support, direct sales services, and specialized services for customers with special needs including visual, hearing, and mobility support.

faq.question: What makes Arab Bank Egypt unique?
faq.answer: Arab Bank Egypt stands out through its connection to one of the Arab world's oldest and most established banking institutions, bringing nearly a century of banking heritage and expertise to the Egyptian market. The bank's unique "Arabi Points Program" offers customers rewards and benefits across their banking relationships. Its digital account offerings, particularly the tiered interest accounts managed through mobile banking, represent modern approaches to savings products. As part of the Arab Bank Group's extensive international network, Egyptian clients gain access to global banking capabilities and can leverage the bank's presence across major financial centers including London, Dubai, Singapore, Geneva, Paris, Frankfurt, and Sydney. This international connectivity makes Arab Bank Egypt particularly valuable for clients engaged in international trade and cross-border transactions.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Arab Bank Egypt?
faq.answer: Arab Bank Egypt operates as a universal bank (or full-service commercial bank) in Egypt. This classification reflects its comprehensive service offering that spans retail banking, corporate banking, SME financing, treasury services, and investment banking capabilities. The bank is registered with the Central Bank of Egypt and operates under Egyptian banking regulations. As a branch network of Arab Bank PLC, it benefits from the parent institution's strong capital position and international banking standards while serving the specific needs of the Egyptian market.

faq.question: How does Arab Bank Egypt support accessibility?
faq.answer: Arab Bank Egypt demonstrates a commitment to inclusive banking through specialized services for customers with disabilities. The bank provides dedicated support for clients with visual, hearing, and mobility challenges, ensuring that banking services are accessible to all segments of Egyptian society. This focus on accessibility extends to the bank's digital platforms, customer service centers, and physical branch network, reflecting modern banking standards for financial inclusion.
